already_told_agent: s01_video_script: The intro should hook viewers in the first 10 seconds and be punchier, possibly starting with a quirky or relatable line.
s01_video_script: Avoid words like “leverage” and “utilise”; use normal, conversational language.
s01_video_script: Break the script into smaller beats to allow for pauses and avoid long paragraphs.
s01_video_script: For each section, provide b-roll suggestions to help plan visuals.
s01_video_script: Add short, personal anecdotes or real moments after each tool to make the script more relatable and engaging.
s01_video_script: Include fun, slightly embarrassing glimpses alongside tool demos to keep the tone cozy but real, and pepper these in for all the tools.
s02_content_calendar: The user prefers a more visual weekly schedule mockup that resembles their Notion dashboard, not just a bullet list or table.
s02_content_calendar: The user does not want a daily posting schedule and values a slower, thoughtful rhythm with quality over quantity.
s02_content_calendar: The user wants each content post clearly numbered or labeled with icons (like circle emojis) for at-a-glance clarity.
s02_content_calendar: The user appreciates the use of colored blocks to visually distinguish busy and chill days.

s02_content_calendar: The user wants the calendar anchored around practical, approachable content that is instantly actionable and cozy, especially posts like “DIY Spring Workspace Upgrades Under $25” and “AI Mood Board Workflows with Canva & Express.”
s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user wants the new thumbnails to be visually consistent with their current ones, especially in layout, font, background blur, and earthy vibe.
s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user does not want any dark or techy thumbnails; all should be warm and bright, not dramatic.

s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user uses a white rounded rectangle for text backgrounds, with clean bold fonts like Montserrat, and prefers text placement consistent with their other commentary-style thumbnails.

s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user prefers subtle graphic elements (like logos or outlines) at low opacity (e.g., CapCut logo at 60% opacity, dashed outlines at 40–50% opacity) and soft, feathered edges.
s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user likes to use background blur (e.g., Canva Pro's Auto Focus at 40%) to create depth while keeping the subject sharp.
s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user uses warm beige or earthy color blocks behind text, with high corner roundness (65–80) and moderate padding for a pill-shaped look.
s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user prefers muted gold or yellow for subtle graphic accents (like sparkles), with low opacity (around 20%).
s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user sometimes adds a barely-there brown overlay at low transparency (about 5%) for an extra earthy glaze.
s03_thumbnail_concepts: The user wants any CapCut logo included to be small, subtle, and at 60% opacity, almost like an Easter egg in the corner.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user prefers short and sweet video descriptions, not long essays.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user is fine with either 2–3 tidy paragraphs or a more bullet-style format for descriptions.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user wants the most important keywords included naturally, especially near the start, but not forced.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user prefers using only 2–3 hashtags at the end of the description, grouped together.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user wants #CapCutAI and one or two cozy, creative hashtags included in the template.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user likes bullet points under the main section if the description feels long, to make it easier to skim.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user wants bullet points to sound more human and specific, with tiny stories or real details from the video, not just keywords.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user likes the use of emojis and a lively, inviting tone in the description.
s04_seo_descriptions: The user wants to avoid a "press release" or "SEO robot" tone in descriptions.
s05_instagram_carousel: The user wants the carousel to feel cozy, punchy, and Instagram-y, not like a bullet point Wikipedia entry.
s05_instagram_carousel: The user prefers a scroll-stopping hook for Slide 1, such as “I let Notion AI mess with my actual creative work. Chaos—or low-key genius?”.
s05_instagram_carousel: The user prefers visuals that blend messy-real and handmade vibes, including before/after doodles, fake-bored faces, and sparkly overlays.
s05_instagram_carousel: The user is open to experimenting with weirder formats, like a “choose your own adventure” carousel or diary entry style with illustrated characters.
s05_instagram_carousel: The user wants the caption to be short, human, and not essay-like, with hashtags that suit their niche (#CapCutAI, cozy/AI/creative).
s06_series_brainstorm: The user wants the series concept to feel instantly recognizable as a 'Sofia Makes' thing, with cozy, Sunday-morning, earth-tone vibes.
s06_series_brainstorm: The user prefers a format that highlights what makes their workflow and approach different for creative people, not generic tech content.
s06_series_brainstorm: The user is a solo creator and cannot do complicated weekly episodes with big set builds or elaborate IRL remakes.
s06_series_brainstorm: The user wants the visual side to stay strong but manageable, relying on their existing environment and aesthetic.
s06_series_brainstorm: The user prefers repeatable, low-lift visual elements like b-roll of watching AI ads on the couch, notebook verdicts, and filming the monitor with cozy elements in the shot.
s06_series_brainstorm: The user wants to avoid painting themselves into a corner and prefers a format that is easy to continue long-term.
s06_series_brainstorm: The user likes the idea of filming small, cozy rituals (pouring coffee, petting their cat) as signature cutaways that can be batch-shot and reused.
s06_series_brainstorm: The user prefers framing episodes around real creative tasks rather than just reviewing tools, so each episode maps to something they actually need to try in their projects.
s06_series_brainstorm: The user wants a series name that works as a playlist title and is cozy and on-brand, not generic like 'Weekly AI Tryouts'.
s07_email_newsletter: The user wants the newsletter to sound quick, cozy, and not-perfect, not like a polished brand marketing email.
s07_email_newsletter: The user wants three subject line options suggested for each newsletter.
s07_email_newsletter: The user prefers intros that grab attention, poke fun at themselves, or start mid-chaos before the greeting.
s07_email_newsletter: The user likes including a PS (and sometimes PPS) at the end of newsletters for a more personal, DM-like touch.
s07_email_newsletter: The user is open to rotating the PS between a cozy question, a tiny win, or something unrelated like a cat update.
s08_media_kit: The user wants a media kit that is a single beautiful page in Canva, not a multi-page PDF or PowerPoint deck.
s08_media_kit: The user prefers engagement rate to be highlighted prominently in the stats cluster, almost headline-level, alongside subscriber and view counts.

s08_media_kit: The user wants to include both YouTube engagement stats (like average view duration, comment/like ratio) and a quick Instagram engagement stat (like average saves or replies per post) in the media kit.
s08_media_kit: The user prefers to leave rates off the main media kit and only share pricing after a brand reaches out.
s08_media_kit: The user wants the "About Me" section to sound warm, quick, and conversational, not dry or robotic.
s08_media_kit: The user prefers the "About Me" section to end with a direct invite or vibe statement, rather than a "let me help you" tone.
s09_thumbnail_prompts: The user wants the AI-generated thumbnail to actually look like them and not just a generic portrait.

s09_thumbnail_prompts: The user provided a 'signature vibe blurb' to copy-paste into DALL-E prompts: 'cozy, earth-tone home office, soft natural light, lived-in but tidy, handmade details, leafy houseplants, wooden desk, ceramic mug, and muted linens.'
s09_thumbnail_prompts: The user keeps their aesthetic recipe in Notion and pastes it into every DALL-E prompt to maintain consistency.
s09_thumbnail_prompts: The user wants background details in the thumbnail prompt to include a cozy home office with a plant, books, a ceramic mug, and linen curtains.
s10_holdout_collab: The user wants the collab video to avoid feeling like two stitched vlogs and instead feel like one cohesive adventure, with shared anchor moments and visual threads.
s10_holdout_collab: The user prefers a 'dueling-modes challenge' format where each creator tackles the same task using their own (AI/digital vs. analog) workflow, with process and reaction focus.
s10_holdout_collab: The user wants to include playful, real-life chaos and cozy workspace atmosphere, emphasizing b-roll of snacks, pets, sunlight, and mini-chaos rituals.
s10_holdout_collab: The user wants to add a beat for 'initial reactions/expectations' where each predicts the other's process before starting.
s10_holdout_collab: The user wants b-roll brainstorms listed under each beat, leaning extra hard into cozy and chaotic moments.
s10_holdout_collab: The user wants a b-roll sequence where both purposely copy a silly part of the other's routine for a visual punchline.
s10_holdout_collab: The user wants a quick, IG Reels-style 'hype-down' montage in the final reveal, including outside verdicts from pets, parents, or friends.
s10_holdout_collab: The user prefers the intro to be punchy, cozy, honest, and mildly self-deprecating, setting up the contrast and chaos within the first 40 seconds.
s10_holdout_collab: The user wants the Notion collab checklist to be super visual, checkbox-style, and not too stiff.
